name: Vibhishana
description: Vibhishana is a pivotal character in the Hindu epic Ramayana, renowned for his unwavering righteousness and devotion to dharma. Born into a Rakshasa (demon) family as the younger brother of Ravana, the antagonist of the epic, Vibhishana stood apart due to his virtuous nature. He vehemently opposed Ravana's abduction of Sita, Rama's wife, and urged him to return her honorably, foreseeing the devastating consequences of his actions. When Ravana stubbornly refused and banished him, Vibhishana sought refuge with Rama, offering his allegiance and counsel. Despite skepticism from some of Rama's allies due to his Rakshasa lineage, Rama accepted Vibhishana, recognizing his inherent goodness and unwavering commitment to truth. Vibhishana's knowledge of Rakshasa strengths and weaknesses proved invaluable to Rama's army during the war against Ravana. He played a crucial role in strategy and provided vital information that contributed to Ravana's eventual defeat. After Ravana's death, Rama crowned Vibhishana as the king of Lanka, entrusting him with the responsibility of ruling justly and establishing dharma in the Rakshasa kingdom. Vibhishana's story is a powerful testament to the triumph of good over evil, the importance of righteousness, and the possibility of redemption, even within seemingly irredeemable circumstances. He is revered as a symbol of integrity and unwavering adherence to dharma, even in the face of familial pressure and potential adversity. His devotion to Rama and his commitment to justice make him a respected figure in Hindu mythology.
subtitle: The Righteous Rakshasa King

keyMyths.title: Vibhishana's Defection
keyMyths.description: Vibhishana, disgusted by Ravana's actions, abandons his brother and seeks refuge with Rama. This act demonstrates his commitment to dharma over familial ties and establishes him as a righteous figure.

keyMyths.title: Vibhishana's Counsel
keyMyths.description: During the war, Vibhishana provides crucial strategic advice to Rama, leveraging his knowledge of Rakshasa strengths and weaknesses. His counsel proves invaluable in securing Rama's victory.

keyMyths.title: Coronation as King
keyMyths.description: After Ravana's defeat, Rama crowns Vibhishana as the king of Lanka, recognizing his virtue and entrusting him with establishing dharma. This marks the triumph of good over evil in the Rakshasa kingdom.

sources.author: Valmiki
sources.description: The primary source for the story of Vibhishana.
sources.text: The primary source for the story of Vibhishana.
